title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer found two blocking issues: (1) the Helm ClusterRole template is missing RBAC rules for the `autoscaling` apiGroup, so HPA operations will get 403 errors when deployed via Helm, and (2) the CRD YAML was not regenerated after adding the `AutoscalingSpec` type, so the `spec.autoscaling` field and its validation rules are absent from the CRD schema. There is also a warning (not shown in full), but the core ask is to add the autoscaling RBAC rule to the ClusterRole template and re-run CRD generation.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 911e368 with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=2 critical=0 warning=1 info=3 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## BLOCKING (2)

### B-001: Helm ClusterRole missing autoscaling RBAC rule
**File**: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml:142` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates

**Problem**: The controller declares kubebuilder RBAC markers for autoscaling resources and SetupWithManager owns HorizontalPodAutoscaler, but the Helm ClusterRole template has no autoscaling apiGroup entry. When deployed via Helm, the operator will receive 403 Forbidden on every HPA create/get/update/delete call, silently breaking reconciliation for any Keystone CR with spec.autoscaling set.

**Code**:
```
# clusterrole.yaml ends at line 156 with coordination.k8s.io — no autoscaling rule
```

**Action Required**: Add an autoscaling RBAC rule to the Helm ClusterRole template after the policy rule.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
  # autoscaling - horizontalpodautoscalers (CC-0038)
  - apiGroups:
      - autoscaling
    resources:
      - horizontalpodautoscalers
    verbs:
      - get
      - list
      - watch
      - create
      - update
      - patch
      - delete
```

**Related**: B-002

---

### B-002: CRD YAML not regenerated after API type changes
**File**: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml:1` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes

**Problem**: keystone_types.go adds the AutoscalingSpec struct and the Autoscaling field on KeystoneSpec, and zz_generated.deepcopy.go was regenerated, but the CRD YAML contains no reference to autoscaling. The spec.autoscaling field, the CEL XValidation rule, and all kubebuilder validation markers are not present in the CRD schema. Users submitting a Keystone CR with spec.autoscaling will have the field silently stripped by the API server.

**Code**:
```
# CRD YAML at operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml contains no reference to autoscaling
```

**Action Required**: Run make manifests (or equivalent controller-gen crd command) and include the regenerated CRD YAML in this PR.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Run `make manifests` to regenerate the CRD YAML and commit the updated file.
```

**Related**: B-001

---

## WARNING (1)

### W-001: normalizeMap helper defined twice as inline closure
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/deployment.go:192-197` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Enforce use of shared helpers instead of inline reimplementation

**Problem**: The normalizeMap closure is defined identically inside both EnsurePDB (lines 192-197) and EnsureHPA (lines 273-278) in deployment.go.

**Code**:
```
normalizeMap := func(m map[string]string) map[string]string {
    if len(m) == 0 {
        return nil
    }
    return m
}
```

**Action Required**: Extract to a package-level unexported function and replace both inline closures with direct calls.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
// normalizeMap converts empty maps to nil so apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual
// does not report spurious diffs between nil and empty maps.
func normalizeMap(m map[string]string) map[string]string {
    if len(m) == 0 {
        return nil
    }
    return m
}
```

---

## INFO (3)

### I-001: EnsureDeployment and EnsureService lack the update-guard pattern
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/deployment.go:61-63` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: New helper functions must follow sibling patterns in the same package

**Problem**: EnsureDeployment and EnsureService unconditionally call Update on every reconcile without the snapshot-before-mutation, owner-ref reconciliation, label/annotation merging, normalizeMap, and DeepEqual guard protections that EnsurePDB and EnsureHPA implement. These are pre-existing and not introduced by this PR.

**Code**:
```
// EnsureDeployment (lines 61-63) and EnsureService (lines 124-128) unconditionally call Update
```

**Action Required**: Consider a follow-up to backport the improved update-guard pattern from EnsurePDB/EnsureHPA to EnsureDeployment and EnsureService.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Backport the snapshot-before-mutation and DeepEqual guard pattern from EnsureHPA/EnsurePDB to EnsureDeployment and EnsureService in a follow-up PR.
```

**Related**: W-001

---

### I-002: int32Ptr helper in test file when generic ptr already exists
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/reconcile_hpa_test.go:77` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Use existing utility functions instead of reimplementing them

**Problem**: reconcile_hpa_test.go defines func int32Ptr(v int32) *int32 while the sibling test file deployment_test.go already defines a generic func ptr[T any](v T) *T. They are in different packages so direct reuse is not possible without a shared test-utils package.

**Code**:
```
func int32Ptr(v int32) *int32 { return &v }
```

**Action Required**: Note for consistency if a shared test-utils package is ever introduced. No immediate action required.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
If a shared test-utils package is introduced, consolidate both helpers into a single generic ptr[T any] function.
```

---

### I-003: Documentation avoids hardcoded sub-reconciler count
**File**: `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:1` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: Documentation was updated from 'all five' to 'all', removing the hardcoded count. This is a positive change noted for completeness — no action needed.

**Code**:
```
# text was correctly updated from "all five" to "all"
```

**Action Required**: No action required. This is a positive observation.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
No fix needed — already correct.
```

---

## Summary

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 2 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 1 |
| INFO     | 3 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Do NOT merge until both BLOCKING issues are resolved: (1) add the autoscaling RBAC rule to the Helm ClusterRole template, and (2) regenerate the CRD YAML with make manifests. The WARNING-level normalizeMap duplication should ideally be fixed in this PR. INFO items can be addressed in follow-ups.
resolution_summary: All three actionable review findings (B-001, B-002, W-001) were addressed. The autoscaling RBAC rule was added to the Helm ClusterRole template, CRD YAMLs were regenerated to include the spec.autoscaling schema with CEL validation, and the duplicated normalizeMap closure was extracted to a package-level function. The CRD regeneration was applied to both operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/ and operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/ (B-002 only mentioned the Helm CRD, but the config/crd/bases copy also needed updating to stay in sync). The normalizeMap extraction in internal/common/deployment/deployment.go removed the inline closure from both EnsurePDB and EnsureHPA, replacing both call sites with the shared helper. INFO items (I-001, I-002, I-003) required no immediate action and were not addressed in this changeset.
